content.text:
Now I can clearly see atom C:6 in the molecule. Looking at the structure, I need to identify which atoms are directly bonded (covalently connected) to atom C:6.

From the image, I can see that atom C:6 is connected to:
- C:1 (carbon atom with index 1)
- C:5 (carbon atom with index 5)
- N:7 (nitrogen atom with index 7)

Let me verify these connections by examining the bonds more carefully. Atom C:6 appears to be part of a ring structure and has three direct bonds: one to C:1, one to C:5, and one to N:7.

The geomconf_index for this molecule appears to be 3649, as indicated by the filename "geomconf_3649.xyz".

So the neighbor atoms of atom C:6 are:
- Atomic index 1, element C
- Atomic index 5, element C  
- Atomic index 7, element N

Geomconf_index: 3649

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 6, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
